refs.description: Rust's SwissTable-based HashMap implementation (the engine behind std::collections::HashMap), using open addressing with SIMD-accelerated group probing.
Google's Abseil flat_hash_map, the original SwissTable open-addressing hash map with control bytes and SSE-based lookup.
Redis core dictionary type backing every keyspace, implemented as an incrementally-rehashing chained hash table.
CPython's built-in dict type, an open-addressing hash table with perturbation probing that underlies object namespaces and kwargs.
